steps.text: Can you turn switch A on?
steps.feedback: A is on! Now turn B on too.
Both on — the lamp lights up! AND needs them both. Turn A off.
The lamp goes out. AND only works when both switches are on.

steps.text: Can you turn switch A on?
steps.feedback: Fan spins! OR only needs one switch. Turn A off.
Fan stops. Now turn B on instead.
B alone works too! OR turns on when either switch is on.

steps.text: Can you turn switch A on?
steps.feedback: Fan spins! XOR works with just one. Turn B on too.
Fan stops! XOR only works when switches are different. Turn B off.
Fan spins again! XOR needs the switches to be different.

steps.text: The lamp is already on! Turn switch A on.
steps.feedback: Lamp goes out! NOT flips the signal. Turn A off again.
Lamp back on! NOT always does the opposite.

steps.text: Turn switches A and B on.
steps.feedback: First AND gate is on! Now turn C and D on too.
All four on — the lamp is lit! Three AND gates, each needs both inputs.
